description: Matching a cluster of confirmed symptoms to a known disease template without being told what disease it is.

drillLibrary.scenario: A 58-year-old male with diabetes presents with sudden onset substernal pressure, diaphoresis, and nausea.
findings: ST-segment elevation in II, III, aVF
drillLibrary.teachingPoint: The combination of pressure, diaphoresis, and jaw radiation in a diabetic is a classic 'textbook' pattern for MI.

drillLibrary.scenario: A 70-year-old female suddenly drops her coffee cup and cannot speak clearly; her right face is drooping.
drillLibrary.teachingPoint: Sudden focal neurological deficits (FAST) are the hallmark pattern of acute ischemic stroke.

drillLibrary.scenario: A 20-year-old male reports periumbilical pain that migrated to the right lower quadrant over 12 hours.
drillLibrary.teachingPoint: Migratory pain from umbilicus to RLQ combined with anorexia is the high-specificity pattern for appendicitis.

drillLibrary.scenario: A 45-year-old woman presents with sudden pleuritic chest pain and shortness of breath 3 days after knee surgery.
drillLibrary.teachingPoint: The triad of sudden dyspnea, pleuritic pain, and a clear lung exam in a post-op patient is highly suggestive of PE.

drillLibrary.scenario: A 75-year-old male presents with productive cough, high fever, and focal crackles in the right lower lobe.
drillLibrary.teachingPoint: Focal lung findings (crackles/dullness) combined with systemic inflammatory signs (fever/WBC) define the pneumonia pattern.

drillLibrary.scenario: A 19-year-old college student presents with severe headache, stiff neck, and photophobia.
drillLibrary.teachingPoint: The 'meningeal triad' of fever, neck stiffness, and altered mental status (or photophobia) is the core diagnostic pattern.

drillLibrary.scenario: A 60-year-old male presents with unilateral calf swelling, redness, and warmth after a long-haul flight.
drillLibrary.teachingPoint: Unilateral leg swelling with a clear provocative history (travel/surgery) is the classic DVT pattern.

drillLibrary.scenario: An 80-year-old female with a UTI becomes confused, hypotensive, and tachypneic.
drillLibrary.teachingPoint: Sepsis is recognized by the pattern of systemic organ dysfunction (qSOFA) in the setting of infection.

drillLibrary.scenario: A 15-year-old with Type 1 DM presents with abdominal pain, vomiting, and deep, rapid breathing.
drillLibrary.teachingPoint: The combination of metabolic acidosis (Kussmaul) and hyperglycemia in a young patient is the DKA pattern.

drillLibrary.scenario: A 25-year-old male develops hives, lip swelling, and wheezing minutes after eating a peanut cookie.
drillLibrary.teachingPoint: Multi-system involvement (skin + respiratory/CV) immediately following an allergen exposure is the anaphylaxis pattern.

drillLibrary.scenario: A 65-year-old female presents with worsening dyspnea, orthopnea, and bilateral ankle edema.
drillLibrary.teachingPoint: Volume overload signs (JVD/edema) plus pulmonary congestion (crackles/orthopnea) define the HF pattern.

drillLibrary.scenario: A 30-year-old female presents with frequency, urgency, and burning during urination.
drillLibrary.teachingPoint: The 'irritative voiding' triad of dysuria, frequency, and urgency in a young female is the primary UTI pattern.

evaluationRubric.correct: Exact match of the disease name (allowing for common abbreviations).
evaluationRubric.partiallyCorrect: Naming the correct organ system or a very close differential (e.g., 'ACS' for MI).

progressionStages.name: Guided Recognition
progressionStages.scaffolding: Key findings are bolded and categorized (e.g., 'Cardiac', 'Respiratory').
progressionStages.interaction: Free text entry with auto-complete hints.

adaptiveRules.prioritization: Drills from failed disease categories appear 2x more frequently.

masteryDefinition.consistency: 100% accuracy across all 12 diseases in Stage 3
